cairosvg requires libcairo2 system library which isn't available on

- Replace _svg_to_png() with async _html_to_png() (Playwright)
- Rename banner_svg → banner_html (now accepts full HTML + CSS)
- Update .fastmcp.yaml install to run playwright install chromium

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: limit RSS items to 25 per feed to reduce scoring overhead

Google News RSS feeds were returning 100+ articles per sector, resulting in
979+ candidates. Corroboration scoring is O(n²), so 979 articles required
~958k comparisons, causing 4+ minute cron runtimes.

Limiting to 25 most recent items per feed reduces candidates to ~250,
cutting scoring time from minutes to seconds while keeping high-quality
recent articles.

This maintains coverage of recent news while preventing excessive scoring
overhead in the validation cron.

commit.message:
add temporary boot diagnostics for headless hang debugging

Three heartbeat services write timestamps to /var/log/boot-diag.log:
PRE-MODULES (before systemd-modules-load), POST-MODULES (after),
NETWORK-UP (after network-online). Tells us exactly which boot phase
hangs when no HDMI is connected.

commit.message:
Parse <tool_code>function(args)</tool_code> tool calls from LLM output

Models like Llama and Qwen emit tool calls as <tool_code> blocks with
function-call syntax instead of structured tool_calls or <tool_call> JSON.
Previously these were treated as plain text, causing the loop to exit
before executing the tool — the root cause of A2A responses ending with
unexecuted tool calls.

- Add _try_parse_tool_code_block() to handle <tool_code>func(args)</tool_code>
  patterns including: string args, kwargs, JSON args, and no args
- Works with mixed content (text + embedded tool_code block) via re.search
- Validates tool names against available_tools to reject hallucinations

commit.message:
Fix auth redirect: use 'redirect' not 'location' in responseOverrides

Azure SWA's responseOverrides schema uses the 'redirect' property (not
'location') to set the Location header on redirects. Using the wrong key
caused a 302 with no Location header → blank page in browser.

commit.message:
fix(infra): add Prisma connection_limit=1 and reduce dev concurrency

- Add ?connection_limit=1 to RDS connection string so each Lambda
  instance holds only 1 DB connection instead of Prisma's default ~5
- Add api_reserved_concurrency variable (default 40, dev override 10)
- Pass reserved_concurrent_executions through to lambda-api module

Root cause: dev API Lambda hit 40 concurrent instances × ~5 pool
connections = ~200 connections against db.t3.micro's 112 limit,
causing "remaining connection slots reserved for SUPERUSER" errors
that blocked migrations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add findHub, repairHub, and always-run repair pass in resumeMigrate

Support scenario where user manually places worktrees at their desired
locations and needs the tool to fix up the .git file connections:

findHub(path): walks upward from any path to find an ancestor hub
(.bare/ + .git). Lets the CLI handle a broken worktree path as input
rather than requiring the exact hub root.

repairHub(dest): admin-dir-first scan of dest/.bare/worktrees/. For each
admin dir whose gitdir points to a worktree inside dest/, checks whether
the worktree's .git file points back to the correct admin dir and fixes it
if not. Uses the admin dir as the source of truth (not the stale .git file)
so it works regardless of how wrong the .git content is.

resumeMigrate: remove the early return on pending.length === 0 — always
run the repairHub pass at the end so worktrees already at the expected
location (whose .git files are stale) also get repaired.

CLI: when source is not a hub but sits inside one (found via findHub),
offer to run repairHub on the ancestor hub instead of a fresh migration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add circuitikz diagrams for N=2 and N=3 in §1.5

- Add \usepackage{circuitikz} and \usetikzlibrary{arrows.meta} to preamble
- N=2 circuit (fig:ckt_N2): 2 lines each R-L series + shunt cap C,
  floating ref P below both lines, v1P/v2P dashed diagonals, vPg,
  current arrows i1/i2. Circuit precedes the 2x2 matrix.
- N=3 circuit (fig:ckt_N3): 3 lines, line 3 has kC (asymmetric),
  v1P/v2P/v3P dashed lines to P below, current arrows i1/i2/i3.
  Circuit precedes the 3x3 matrix.
- Both circuits match Fig.1 topology: R-L series, right-end shunt caps,
  P below the lines, vPg to ground

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: simplify Caddyfile TLS config for reliable ACME challenges

The dual ZeroSSL + Let's Encrypt issuer config prevented Caddy's
ACME challenge handler from intercepting HTTP-01 requests on port 80,
causing challenges to redirect to HTTPS and fail silently. Simplify
to `tls <email>` which uses Caddy's default Let's Encrypt with
working TLS-ALPN-01 challenge support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix icon rendering - use object-contain to show full icon

Changed object-cover to object-contain for application icons in the
table, edit form, and message list. This ensures non-square icons
(like rectangular logos) are fully visible instead of being cropped
to fill the container.

commit.message:
perf: replace brute-force wild card evaluation with analytical approach

Pruned candidate generation reduces wild card assignments from 56^N to
~10-15 per wild. 1-wild: ~15 candidates vs 56. 2-wild: ~225 vs 3,136.

Results: 153 games/sec (up from ~40), 3.8x improvement.
Cross-checked against brute-force on 2000 random hands — identical results.
All 203 tests pass including deterministic reproducibility.
